title: Tools, Device Commands, PBQs, and Certification Capstone · Network Stakeholder Mission
summary: A growing organization connecting a branch, cloud services, and remote operators. The organization needs a complete design, implementation plan, security model, monitoring package, and repaired failure scenario. Planned artifact: Deliver N10-009 Branch Network Capstone with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives.
Solve performance-based configuration and troubleshooting tasks by preserving requirements, recording state, and verifying all paths.
Interpret MAC table, route, interface, configuration, ARP, VLAN, and power command outputs without assuming vendor syntax.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-incident-documentation-11hqm3" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Incident Documentation into observable performance evidence.

content.code:

# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable probable cause]
# command: choose one safe read-only simulator command
# observation: [replace with the result that supports or rejects the hypothesis]

stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: required

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.

Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Net Incident Documentation condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Incident Documentation applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record timeline, environment, commands, findings, actions, outcomes, lessons, and evidence without exposing secrets.
